steps.title: Factor out the GCF
steps.content:
Each term in $3x^2+45x+18$ is divisible by $3$:
$$3x^2+45x+18=3(x^2+15x+6)$$

steps.title: Check that the remaining quadratic is irreducible over integers
steps.content: For $x^2+15x+6$, we need two integers whose product is $6$ and sum is $15$. The only integer pairs giving product $6$ are $(1,6)$, $(2,3)$, $(-1,-6)$, and $(-2,-3)$, none of which sum to $15$. So $3$ is the only clean factor among the listed choices.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $3$: divides every term, so it is a factor. Keep.
  • B) $6x$: $3x^2/6x$ is not a polynomial, so $6x$ is not a factor. Eliminate.
  • C) $45x$: $18/45x$ is not a polynomial, so $45x$ is not a factor. Eliminate.
  • D) $3x^2$: $45x/3x^2$ is not a polynomial, so $3x^2$ is not a factor. Eliminate.
A is correct.
